headline: Argentina defend the crown — Messi's likely final World Cup
summary: Reigning champions Argentina, led by captain Lionel Messi on a historic sixth World Cup appearance, aim to become the first nation since Brazil in 1962 to win back-to-back World Cups. Scaloni kept the core of the 2022 winners (Enzo Fernández, Mac Allister, Julián Álvarez, Lautaro Martínez). Widely framed as Messi's final World Cup; Argentina open in Group J against Algeria, with Austria and Jordan to follow.
angles: Back-to-back titles haven't happened since 1962 — can Argentina break the curse?
Is this REALLY Messi's last dance at a World Cup?
Can Argentina defend the crown if Messi isn't fully fit?
Who carries Argentina when Messi can't — Álvarez, Lautaro, or Enzo?
